Hey guys, JB here. These weekend motivations are really about just giving you cool things to think about, food for thought. Have a great day and let's do some serious butt kicking on Monday. All right? Take care. Tell us about, we want to hear your definition about what tonality is and what's the real outcome of using it in a sales setting? Well, you know, for me, the funny thing about tonality is that, you know, I think as a kid, I thought everyone had the same tonality that I did in terms of the ability to use tonality to persuade and to get your point across. You know, I always love to use the example as a movie called Ferris Buell's Day Off. And there's a great scene in the movie where the teacher, I can't remember the guy's name, he's a great actor, he's very dry and he's actually being all monotone in front of the class. And he's like, okay, class, open up your book, you know, we're going to talk about the Smoot Hall, the Act. And as he's talking in this unbelievably monotone, meaning one tonality, and it's going to sort of same pace, you watch the kids like to start nodding off when kids drooling. And when we see that, we laugh because we know it's funny because we've all have been on the wrong end of someone like that. It was boring and his tone less than essentially no matter how great their message might be, we don't hear it. We tune out. One of the things about the human nervous system, the way it's built, the way we move through the world is we tend to habituate you very quickly on a stimulus that is the same. So in other words, if I'm sitting here right now, yes, the air is moving onto my skin. And when I first walked in the room, I was actually aware that it's a little bit cold in here. But now within five minutes, my skin no longer even feels that it's still cold, but I habituated. So my nervous system blocks it out so I can focus on other information that's more important and more relevant to what's going on here. See, our conscious mind only has a limited amount of processing power, while our unconscious mind has massive unlimited processing power. So what we do is we block out the stuff that's unimportant. Well, guess what? If you try to talk to someone and you have really important information that can change their lives, but you do it in a way that causes them to habituate, meaning you're tone less or you're, you know, or you're almost tone less and you just, you know, sort of drag on people will tune you out and you can't get your point across at all. That's an example of having awful, like, you know, the worst tonality, right? As was in that movie, right? But then that's just the continuum. That's like the worst tonality possible. And let's say there's someone like me who's really mastered the art of tonality, where I know how to like, you know, sound enthusiastic, whether I really am or not, I can tell you anything like it's a secret or, you know, move my body and get people interested and have some of these really, these amazing competencies that we're going to discuss, these core competencies with tonalities and impact, enthusiasm, you know, which I'm sure you're going to get to. But so if I get represent one end of the continuum, which is unbelievable tonality, and then the teacher from Ferris-Grula represents terrible tonality, then we have everybody who's watching this. So all you are watching and learning here, they're somewhere in between. You know, some people are really naturally great with tonality and others are monotone. So the thing I would say about tonality, interesting part is that no matter how bad your tonality might be, you, you, it doesn't really matter. At some point in your life, you've used every tonality that is required to become a master of the straight line. In other words, there's only 10 tonalities that you have to learn to essentially muster at will. Right. And we'll get into them a little bit later in this training here, but the point is this, you don't have to learn how to use them for the first time in your life. You've already used them, but you use them by accident. You use them randomly when you actually felt a certain way. There was once in your life where you did feel absolutely certain. So of course you talked in a tone of absolute certainty. There was a time where you felt really sincere and empathetic and you were able to talk in the tone of sincerity. There was a time when you felt that you were being really, really reasonable and you looked at someone and said, you know, I'm reasonable. You're reasonable. Isn't that fair enough? Come on, honestly. And you had the tone of the ultimate reasonable man. Right. So those are some of the key tones we need to become influencers and we've all used them from time to time.
